summary: A small symmetrical courtyard fortress with a cruciform raised central platform and two tall corner watchtowers connected to stepped, layered perimeter walls. The build uses alternating horizontal bands of a yellowish wood planks and darker log/wood vertical supports, capped with stone brick slabs and slabs of a darker material on top of the central platform and tower roofs. A low stonebrick boundary wall surrounds the site with spaced stone slab posts; ground is bare dirt/terracotta.

shape.overall_form: central cruciform raised platform with surrounding stepped walls and two tall corner towers
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along central cross axis (mirrored left/right)

materials.name: nether_brick_or_dark
materials.role: dark top surface of central cross and tower roofs (uncertain exact dark block)

materials.name: glowstone
materials.role: lighting on corners and tower tops

elements: central cruciform raised platform (dark top, stonebrick edging)
two tall watchtowers with overhanging flat roofs
stepped layered perimeter walls composed of horizontal wood bands and stonebrick ledges
low outer stonebrick slab fence posts forming outer boundary
glowstone lights on parapets and tower roofs
rebuild_hints: Lay an outer rectangular boundary of spaced stone slab blocks (~11x11 spacing from center) and a 1-block-high continuous stonebrick slab curb around the build area.

Construct a raised central cross-shaped platform elevated 2-3 blocks above ground with a dark block top (nether-brick-like) and stonebrick slab edging; arms of the cross extend north/south/east/west symmetrically.

Around the central platform build stepped layered walls: each wall section is composed of vertical log corner/strip supports, three horizontal bands of wood planks separated by stonebrick slab ledges; repeat height steps toward corners.

Place two tall square towers on opposite sides (diagonally offset) using vertical logs with small window gaps and an overhanging flat roof made from stone slabs with dark block center and glowstone on top.
Use small stonebrick stair/ slab detailing on each step of the wall tiers to match the visible ledges, and leave the courtyard interior open (dirt) with no roof.
uncertainties: Exact block type used for the dark reddish top (appears like nether brick or dark clay) is uncertain.
Ground material inside courtyard looks like coarse dirt/terracotta; exact block identity uncertain.
Precise footprint dimensions are estimated from images and may be off by a few blocks.
Number of internal floors/accessible interior levels inside towers and walls is not visible; floors_estimate is approximate.
